guardrail: static projection of the public register; no facts added here

data.name: United States -> Japan (USD->JPY settlement)

divergence.reason: Directed-edge operative interaction sets (Corridor Atlas v0.2.5 §5, read at the destination inbound gate for THIS direction) differ from the undirected pairwise sets (Architecture §5.14). The Atlas generalizes the §5.12/§5.14 undirected pairs into directed edges and re-derives the operative interaction sets per direction; the compatibility CATEGORY agrees, the interaction-set list may differ by direction. This divergence is declared, not silent.

infrastructure_overlap.note: Both ends on Project Agora (US and JP).

inbound_mechanism.test: Japan Electronic Payment Instruments inbound channel-determination
inbound_mechanism.detail: Foreign token circulates only via a registered EPIESP holding reserves in Japan equal to customer holdings, with the foreign issuer not issuing/redeeming/soliciting to Japanese users (recognition + registration, not mutual recognition).

data.note: A dollar issuer reaching into Japan does not meet automatic dual authorization; it meets a channel. A foreign-issued stablecoin circulates only via a registered EPIESP holding reserves in Japan equal to customer holdings, while the foreign issuer does not issue/redeem/solicit to Japanese users. The working example is USDC, admitted via SBI VC Trade (March 2025). Because the gating step is a recognition + registration channel-determination rather than mutual recognition, the corridor is Category II — the same class as entry into the US or the UAE — even though Japan is a fully live regime.

source.primary: Fan, Cross-Border Digital-Finance Corridor Atlas v0.2.5 (June 2026), §5.18 (United States -> Japan)
source.secondary: Fan, Cross-Border Stablecoin Architecture v3 (Twelve Jurisdictions, June 2026), §5.14 (US x JP: Category I/II hybrid — undirected §5.14 pair; see this edge's `divergence` field)

class_basis.instrument: PSA Electronic Payment Instruments channel through a registered EPIESP (reserves in Japan; foreign-issuer non-solicitation). Cabinet Office Ordinance promulgated 2026-05-19, in force 2026-06-01, recognises equivalent foreign trust-type stablecoins as electronic payment instruments, subject to (i) an equivalent home licence, (ii) appropriately managed and audited reserves, and (iii) an information-sharing / cooperation arrangement between the home regulator and the JFSA.
